elements.name: cui-article-filter
elements.description:
Filters a selector-targeted list of article cards by category. Reads
`data-category` from each `.cui-article-card` and shows/hides cards based on
which radio/checkbox inputs inside the filter are checked.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-article-filter-change** - Fires when the visible-category set changes. `detail.selected` is the array of currently checked values.

### **Methods:**
 - **refresh(): _void_** - Re-read children and recompute visibility. Call after dynamic updates.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Radio or checkbox inputs (typically wrapped in labels). Each input's `value` is matched against the `data-category` on the target cards.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: CSS selector for the element containing the cards. Required: if it is omitted, or it matches no element, the filter no-ops and warns in the console.

slots.description: Radio or checkbox inputs (typically wrapped in labels). Each input's `value` is matched against the `data-category` on the target cards.

events.name: cui-article-filter-change
events.description: Fires when the visible-category set changes. `detail.selected` is the array of currently checked values.

properties.name: selectedValues
properties.description: Selected category values from checked radios / checkboxes inside the filter.

events.name: cui-article-filter-change
events.description: Fires when the visible-category set changes. `detail.selected` is the array of currently checked values.

elements.name: cui-button
elements.description:
Renders a `<button>` or `<a>` with shared visual styling and disabled state
handling. Choose anchor mode by setting the `href` attribute, and button mode by omitting it.
---

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: = Disables the underlying control. For anchor mode this also prevents navigation.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: When set, the button renders as an anchor (`<a>`) instead of a `<button>`. The value is applied to the anchor's `href` attribute.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Anchor target (e.g. `_blank`). Only applies when `href` is set.

attributes.name: rel
attributes.description: Anchor relationship. Only applies when `href` is set.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Button type. Only applies when `href` is not set. Defaults to `"button"`.

elements.name: cui-code
elements.description:
Displays a code sample with a copy-to-clipboard button. Reads source from
its text content, from an HTML comment child, or from a `<script type="…">`
child — whichever is present — so example markup can stay valid HTML.
---


### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - The code to display. Can be raw text, an HTML comment containing markup, or a `<script type="text/plain">` block (useful when the example itself is HTML you don't want the browser to render).

attributes.name: language
attributes.description: Source language hint (`text`, `html`, `css`, `js`, …). Used as a CSS hook and in the copy-button label.

slots.description: The code to display. Can be raw text, an HTML comment containing markup, or a `<script type="text/plain">` block (useful when the example itself is HTML you don't want the browser to render).

elements.name: cui-day-planner
elements.description:
Vertical day planner — a single-day timeline that decorates a
slotted list of `.cui-event-card` children. Events are placed
by start time, sized by duration, and column-packed so
concurrent events sit side-by-side. The source cards remain in
light DOM beneath the board (or visually hidden via
`events-hidden`) so search engines and feed readers index the
full event copy without depending on the component's JS.

Reads each card's first `<time datetime>` for the start; an
optional second `<time datetime>` or a `data-end` attribute
(full ISO or `HH:MM`) provides the end. Cards without an
explicit end get a default `60`-minute duration.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-day-planner-event-select** - Cancelable; fires when an event block is activated.
- **cui-day-planner-slot-select** - Fires when a row (empty slot) is activated.
- **cui-day-planner-navigate** - Fires after the visible day changes.

### **Methods:**
 - **goTo(target: _string | Date_): _void_** - Programmatically jump to a date (YYYY-MM-DD or Date).
- **next(): _void_** - Move forward one day.
- **previous(): _void_** - Move back one day.
- **refresh(): _void_** - Re-read slotted event cards. Call after dynamic updates.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - `.cui-event-card` elements whose first `<time>` falls on the visible date. Cards on other days are placed in the slot but skipped from the timeline.

attributes.name: date
attributes.description: Visible day in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Defaults to today.

attributes.name: start-hour
attributes.description: Visible window start hour (0-23). Defaults to `7`.

attributes.name: end-hour
attributes.description: Visible window end hour (1-24). Defaults to `22`.

attributes.name: slot-minutes
attributes.description: Minor-gridline cadence. Defaults to `60`.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description: BCP-47 locale for the day-header label. Defaults to the document's `lang` or `navigator.language`.

attributes.name: now-line
attributes.description: Render the now-line when viewing today. Defaults to present.

attributes.name: events-hidden
attributes.description: Visually hide the slotted card list while keeping it in the DOM.

slots.description: `.cui-event-card` elements whose first `<time>` falls on the visible date. Cards on other days are placed in the slot but skipped from the timeline.

events.name: cui-day-planner-event-select
events.description: Cancelable; fires when an event block is activated.

events.name: cui-day-planner-slot-select
events.description: Fires when a row (empty slot) is activated.

events.name: cui-day-planner-navigate
events.description: Fires after the visible day changes.

events.name: cui-day-planner-event-select
events.description: Cancelable; fires when an event block is activated.

events.name: cui-day-planner-slot-select
events.description: Fires when a row (empty slot) is activated.

events.name: cui-day-planner-navigate
events.description: Fires after the visible day changes.

elements.name: cui-calendar
elements.description:
Month-view calendar that decorates a slotted list of
`.cui-event-card` children. Reads each card's first `<time
datetime>` to plot an event pill onto the matching day cell;
the source cards remain in light DOM beneath the grid (or
visually hidden via `events-hidden`) so search engines and feed
readers index the full event copy without depending on the
component's JS.

Click a day to fire `cui-calendar-day-select`; click an event
pill to fire `cui-calendar-event-select`; the prev / next
buttons fire `cui-calendar-navigate`. Keyboard nav inside the
grid uses arrow keys, Home / End, and PgUp / PgDn (with Shift
jumping a year).
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-calendar-event-select** - Fires when an event pill is activated.
- **cui-calendar-day-select** - Fires when a day cell is activated.
- **cui-calendar-navigate** - Fires when the visible month changes.

### **Methods:**
 - **goTo(year: _number_, month: _number_): _void_** - Programmatically navigate to a specific month (1-12).
- **next(): _void_** - Move forward one month.
- **previous(): _void_** - Move back one month.
- **refresh(): _void_** - Re-read slotted event cards. Call after dynamic updates.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - One or more `.cui-event-card` elements. Cards without a `<time datetime>` are skipped; multi-day events use the start date for plotting (single-cell rendering for v1).

attributes.name: year
attributes.description: Initial visible year (e.g. `"2026"`). Defaults to today.

attributes.name: month
attributes.description: Initial visible month (1-12). Defaults to today.

attributes.name: weekday-start
attributes.description: First day of the week. Defaults to `"monday"`.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description: BCP-47 locale used for month / weekday labels. Defaults to the document's `lang` or `navigator.language`.

attributes.name: events-hidden
attributes.description: Visually hide the source event list while keeping it in the DOM for accessibility and SEO.

slots.description: One or more `.cui-event-card` elements. Cards without a `<time datetime>` are skipped; multi-day events use the start date for plotting (single-cell rendering for v1).

events.name: cui-calendar-event-select
events.description: Fires when an event pill is activated.

events.name: cui-calendar-day-select
events.description: Fires when a day cell is activated.

events.name: cui-calendar-navigate
events.description: Fires when the visible month changes.

events.name: cui-calendar-event-select
events.description: Fires when an event pill is activated.

events.name: cui-calendar-day-select
events.description: Fires when a day cell is activated.

events.name: cui-calendar-navigate
events.description: Fires when the visible month changes.

elements.name: cui-carousel
elements.description: Flexible content carousel. Slides are slotted light-DOM elements, so every slide stays crawlable regardless of which one is visible. Compose each slide from any markup — an `` / `

attributes.name: index
attributes.description: The active slide index (zero-based). Reflected.

attributes.name: autoplay
attributes.description: Advance slides automatically.

attributes.name: interval
attributes.description: Autoplay delay in milliseconds. Defaults to 5000.

attributes.name: loop
attributes.description: Wrap past the ends. Defaults to on; set `loop="false"` to clamp at the first / last slide and disable the wrap.

attributes.name: transition
attributes.description: Transition style. Defaults to `slide`.

attributes.name: controls
attributes.description: Show the previous / next arrows. Defaults to on; set `controls="false"` to hide them.

attributes.name: pagination
attributes.description: Show the dot pagination. Defaults to on; set `pagination="false"` to hide it.

attributes.name: pause-on-hover
attributes.description: Pause autoplay on hover / focus. Defaults to on; set `pause-on-hover="false"` to keep playing.

slots.name: slide
slots.description: One element per slide. Any content works; use `.cui-media-overlay` for media with overlaid copy and buttons.

slots.name: fixed
slots.description: Content overlaid on top of every slide and pinned in place while slides change (logo, persistent call-to-action). Buttons here can carry `data-cui-carousel-prev` / `-next` / `-goto="<i>"` to drive the carousel; elements anywhere else on the page can do the same by adding `data-cui-carousel-target="<id>"`.

events.name: cui-carousel-change
events.description: Fires after the active slide changes.

properties.name: slides
properties.description: All slide elements, in document order.

properties.name: playing
properties.description: Whether autoplay is currently advancing slides.

events.name: cui-carousel-change
events.description: Fires after the active slide changes.

elements.name: cui-disclosure
elements.description:
Lightweight custom element that wraps a native `<details>` element so it can
be opened, closed, or toggled by ID from anywhere on the page, and so its
open state is observable as an attribute and a custom event.

The `<details>` element stays in the light DOM, so it's contents remain
searchable, indexable, and stylable from the host page.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-disclosure-toggle** - Bubbles after the disclosure opens/closes. `detail.open` is the new state.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - A single `<details>` element with its `<summary>` and body. The element does not render its own UI - it only observes and controls the nested `<details>`.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Reflects the inner `<details>.open`. Set or remove this attribute to open/close the disclosure; the attribute also updates when the user toggles the disclosure by clicking the `<summary>` directly.

slots.description: A single `<details>` element with its `<summary>` and body. The element does not render its own UI - it only observes and controls the nested `<details>`.

events.name: cui-disclosure-toggle
events.description: Bubbles after the disclosure opens/closes. `detail.open` is the new state.

events.name: cui-disclosure-toggle
events.description: Bubbles after the disclosure opens/closes. `detail.open` is the new state.

elements.name: cui-field
elements.description:
Form-field wrapper that surfaces native `ValidityState` as user-visible
messages, supports server-side errors via `setError()`, async validators
via `addValidator()`, and i18n via slotted `error-<validityKey>` content.
Designed to be a child of `cui-form`, but works standalone.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-field-valid** - Fires when the field transitions to a valid state.
- **cui-field-invalid** - Fires when validation fails. `detail.key` is the first failing `ValidityState` key; `detail.params` carries control-derived values for message interpolation.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - The form control (`<input>`, `<select>`, `<textarea>`, or a custom control matching `CuiFieldControl`).
- **label** - The field label. Will be associated with the control via `for=` / `aria-labelledby`.
- **help** - Descriptive help text shown beneath the control.
- **error** - Generic error message. Used when no `error-<key>` slot matches the failing validity key.
- **error-{validityKey}** - Validity-key-specific error message (`error-valueMissing`, `error-typeMismatch`, `error-tooShort`, …). The first matching slot wins.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Mirrors the control's `required` attribute and marks the field visually as required.

slots.description: The form control (`<input>`, `<select>`, `<textarea>`, or a custom control matching `CuiFieldControl`).

slots.name: label
slots.description: The field label. Will be associated with the control via `for=` / `aria-labelledby`.

slots.name: help
slots.description: Descriptive help text shown beneath the control.

slots.name: error
slots.description: Generic error message. Used when no `error-<key>` slot matches the failing validity key.

slots.name: error-{validityKey}
slots.description: Validity-key-specific error message (`error-valueMissing`, `error-typeMismatch`, `error-tooShort`, …). The first matching slot wins.

events.name: cui-field-valid
events.description: Fires when the field transitions to a valid state.

events.name: cui-field-invalid
events.description: Fires when validation fails. `detail.key` is the first failing `ValidityState` key; `detail.params` carries control-derived values for message interpolation.

events.name: cui-field-valid
events.description: Fires when the field transitions to a valid state.

events.name: cui-field-invalid
events.description: Fires when validation fails. `detail.key` is the first failing `ValidityState` key; `detail.params` carries control-derived values for message interpolation.

elements.name: cui-cookie-banner
elements.description:
Cookie consent banner. Renders a fixed bottom-anchored card with an
accept-all / necessary-only / settings flow, persists the choice in a
cookie, and emits a typed `cui-cookie-consent-change` event so the host
app can load (or block) analytics and marketing scripts. The component is
deliberately tracker-free — it manages consent and UI only; you wire the
actual scripts in response to the event.

On connect it reads the stored cookie: when absent the banner opens; when
present it stays hidden and re-emits the saved consent with
`reason: "restore"` so consumers can re-apply it on every page load.

All copy is provided through named slots with English defaults, so the
banner is fully translatable and brandable without touching the component.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-cookie-banner-open** - Fires after the banner opens.
- **cui-cookie-banner-close** - Fires after the banner closes.
- **cui-cookie-consent-change** - Fires whenever consent is saved, revoked, or restored from the cookie.

### **Methods:**
 - **revoke(): _void_** - Clear the stored choice and re-open the banner.

### **Slots:**
 - **title** - Banner heading. Default "Cookie settings".
- **description** - Intro paragraph explaining cookie use.
- **accept-label** - Accept-all button text. Default "Accept all".
- **decline-label** - Necessary-only button text. Default "Necessary only".
- **settings-label** - Open-settings button text. Default "Settings".
- **save-label** - Save-preferences button text. Default "Save preferences".
- **necessary-label** - / necessary-description - Necessary category copy.
- **analytics-label** - / analytics-description - Analytics category copy.
- **marketing-label** - / marketing-description - Marketing category copy.
- **policy** - Cookie policy link. Default points to `/privacy-policy`.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Reflects banner visibility. Set or remove to show/hide imperatively.

attributes.name: categories
attributes.description: Comma-separated optional categories to show (subset of `analytics,marketing`). Default shows both. `necessary` is always present and locked on.

attributes.name: manual
attributes.description: When present, the banner does not auto-open on connect even without a stored choice. Call `show()` to open it yourself.

attributes.name: cookie-name
attributes.description: Name of the consent cookie. Default `cui-cookie-consent`.

attributes.name: cookie-expiry
attributes.description: Cookie lifetime in days. Default `365`.

slots.name: title
slots.description: Banner heading. Default "Cookie settings".

slots.name: description
slots.description: Intro paragraph explaining cookie use.

slots.name: accept-label
slots.description: Accept-all button text. Default "Accept all".

slots.name: decline-label
slots.description: Necessary-only button text. Default "Necessary only".

slots.name: settings-label
slots.description: Open-settings button text. Default "Settings".

slots.name: save-label
slots.description: Save-preferences button text. Default "Save preferences".

slots.name: necessary-label
slots.description: / necessary-description - Necessary category copy.

slots.name: analytics-label
slots.description: / analytics-description - Analytics category copy.

slots.name: marketing-label
slots.description: / marketing-description - Marketing category copy.

slots.name: policy
slots.description: Cookie policy link. Default points to `/privacy-policy`.

events.name: cui-cookie-banner-open
events.description: Fires after the banner opens.

events.name: cui-cookie-banner-close
events.description: Fires after the banner closes.

events.name: cui-cookie-consent-change
events.description: Fires whenever consent is saved, revoked, or restored from the cookie.

properties.name: categories
properties.description: Optional categories currently shown, derived from the `categories` attribute.

properties.name: consent
properties.description: The current stored consent, or `null` when no choice has been made.

events.name: cui-cookie-banner-open
events.description: Fires after the banner opens.

events.name: cui-cookie-banner-close
events.description: Fires after the banner closes.

events.name: cui-cookie-consent-change
events.description: Fires whenever consent is saved, revoked, or restored from the cookie.

elements.name: cui-form
elements.description:
Coordinates submission and validation across child `cui-field` elements.
Runs each field's async validators, blocks submit on failures, and
surfaces submit / success / error events with typed details. Set a JS
submit handler with `setSubmitHandler(fn)`.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-form-invalid** - Fires when submission is blocked because one or more fields failed validation.
- **cui-form-submit** - Fires when the form passes validation and is about to submit. `detail.formData` is the `FormData` snapshot.
- **cui-form-success** - Fires after a JS submit handler resolves successfully.
- **cui-form-error** - Fires when a JS submit handler rejects. `detail.error` is the thrown value.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - A native `<form>` element containing `cui-field`s and a submit button. The form's `action` and `method` are honored when no JS handler is attached.

slots.description: A native `<form>` element containing `cui-field`s and a submit button. The form's `action` and `method` are honored when no JS handler is attached.

events.name: cui-form-invalid
events.description: Fires when submission is blocked because one or more fields failed validation.

events.name: cui-form-submit
events.description: Fires when the form passes validation and is about to submit. `detail.formData` is the `FormData` snapshot.

events.name: cui-form-success
events.description: Fires after a JS submit handler resolves successfully.

events.name: cui-form-error
events.description: Fires when a JS submit handler rejects. `detail.error` is the thrown value.

events.name: cui-form-invalid
events.description: Fires when submission is blocked because one or more fields failed validation.

events.name: cui-form-submit
events.description: Fires when the form passes validation and is about to submit. `detail.formData` is the `FormData` snapshot.

events.name: cui-form-success
events.description: Fires after a JS submit handler resolves successfully.

events.description: Fires when a JS submit handler rejects. `detail.error` is the thrown value.

elements.name: cui-map
elements.description:
Leaflet-backed interactive map with clustered points, GeoJSON regions,
theme-aware tiles, and a lazy-loaded library bundle. Points and regions
are declared as light-DOM children so content stays SEO-visible and
server-renderable.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-map-ready** - Fires once the library has loaded and the map is interactive.
- **cui-map-bounds-change** - Fires after the user pans or zooms.
- **cui-map-point-click** - Fires when a marker is clicked.
- **cui-map-region-click** - Fires when a GeoJSON region is clicked.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Fallback content shown before the library loads or when JS is disabled (e.g. a static `<address>` list).
- **title** - Accessible title for the map (becomes an `aria-label`).
- **legend** - Visual legend rendered alongside the map.
- **marker-icon** - Template for custom marker icons. Cloned per point.

attributes.name: center
attributes.description: Initial center as `"lat,lng"` (e.g. `52.37,4.89`).

attributes.name: zoom
attributes.description: Initial zoom level.

attributes.name: min-zoom
attributes.description: Minimum zoom level allowed.

attributes.name: max-zoom
attributes.description: Maximum zoom level allowed.

attributes.name: tile-url
attributes.description: Tile URL template. Defaults to OpenStreetMap.

attributes.name: tile-attribution
attributes.description: HTML attribution string for the tiles.

attributes.name: tile-subdomains
attributes.description: Comma-separated subdomain list for the tile URL template.

attributes.name: cluster
attributes.description: Group nearby markers into clusters.

attributes.name: fit-bounds
attributes.description: Auto-zoom to fit all points/regions on load.

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description: Enable pan/zoom (default true).

attributes.name: scroll-wheel-zoom
attributes.description: Enable scroll-wheel zoom.

slots.description: Fallback content shown before the library loads or when JS is disabled (e.g. a static `<address>` list).

slots.name: title
slots.description: Accessible title for the map (becomes an `aria-label`).

slots.name: legend
slots.description: Visual legend rendered alongside the map.

slots.name: marker-icon
slots.description: Template for custom marker icons. Cloned per point.

events.name: cui-map-ready
events.description: Fires once the library has loaded and the map is interactive.

events.name: cui-map-bounds-change
events.description: Fires after the user pans or zooms.

events.name: cui-map-point-click
events.description: Fires when a marker is clicked.

events.name: cui-map-region-click
events.description: Fires when a GeoJSON region is clicked.

elements.name: cui-reveal
elements.description:
Reveals its content with an entry animation as it scrolls into view, using
`IntersectionObserver` under the hood. Honors `prefers-reduced-motion` —
users with reduced motion see the content immediately, no transform.
---


### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Content to reveal. Any block-level content works.

attributes.name: delay
attributes.description: Delay before the reveal starts. Accepts a number (milliseconds) or any CSS time value (`200ms`, `0.3s`).

attributes.name: distance
attributes.description: Translate distance for the entry transform. Accepts a number (pixels) or any CSS length (`24px`, `2rem`).

attributes.name: threshold
attributes.description: `IntersectionObserver` threshold (0–1).

slots.description: Content to reveal. Any block-level content works.

elements.name: cui-modal
elements.description:
Modal dialog built over the native `<dialog>` element. Backdrop dismiss,
ESC handling, and focus management come from the platform; this element
adds attribute control, an open/close JS API, typed close events, and
remote triggering via `data-cui-modal-target` from anywhere on the page.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-modal-open** - Fires after the dialog opens.
- **cui-modal-close** - Fires after the dialog closes. `detail.returnValue` carries the dialog's `returnValue` (set via `close(value)` or `<form method="dialog">`).
- **cui-modal-cancel** - Fires when the user cancels via ESC or backdrop click (before `cui-modal-close`).

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - The dialog body. Wrap with a `<div>` or semantic landmark; include a close affordance (any element with `data-cui-modal-close`) for users who can't dismiss via ESC.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Reflects the dialog's open state. Set or remove to open/close imperatively.

slots.description: The dialog body. Wrap with a `<div>` or semantic landmark; include a close affordance (any element with `data-cui-modal-close`) for users who can't dismiss via ESC.

events.name: cui-modal-open
events.description: Fires after the dialog opens.

events.name: cui-modal-close
events.description: Fires after the dialog closes. `detail.returnValue` carries the dialog's `returnValue` (set via `close(value)` or `<form method="dialog">`).

events.name: cui-modal-cancel
events.description: Fires when the user cancels via ESC or backdrop click (before `cui-modal-close`).

events.name: cui-modal-open
events.description: Fires after the dialog opens.

events.name: cui-modal-close
events.description: Fires after the dialog closes. `detail.returnValue` carries the dialog's `returnValue` (set via `close(value)` or `<form method="dialog">`).

events.name: cui-modal-cancel
events.description: Fires when the user cancels via ESC or backdrop click (before `cui-modal-close`).

elements.name: cui-tabs
elements.description:
Tabbed interface with ARIA `tablist`/`tab`/`tabpanel` roles and full
keyboard support (Left/Right, Home/End). The element renders no styling of
its own — it enhances light-DOM markup built from the `.cui-tabs`,
`.cui-tablist`, and `.cui-tab` classes, wiring up `id`, `aria-controls`,
`aria-selected`, roles, and panel visibility. All panels stay in the light
DOM so crawlers see every panel regardless of which tab is active.

Tabs are the `.cui-tab` controls inside the `.cui-tablist`; panels are the
remaining direct children of the element, paired with the tabs by source
order. Mark the initially-open tab with `aria-selected="true"` (defaults to
the first tab).
---

elements.name: cui-theme-toggle
elements.description:
Button that cycles the page theme through `auto`, `light`, and `dark`. The
choice is persisted in `localStorage` under `cui-theme` and read at boot
(see the inline script in the docs `<head>`). Pairs with the exported
`getTheme()` / `setTheme()` helpers for programmatic control.
---

attributes.name: label-auto
attributes.description: Visible label when in auto mode.

attributes.name: label-light
attributes.description: Visible label when in light mode.

attributes.name: label-dark
attributes.description: Visible label when in dark mode.

attributes.name: aria-label-auto
attributes.description: Accessible name when in auto mode.

attributes.name: aria-label-light
attributes.description: Accessible name when in light mode.

attributes.name: aria-label-dark
attributes.description: Accessible name when in dark mode.

elements.name: cui-toast-region
elements.description:
Container for transient toast notifications. Usually created on demand by
the exported `toast()` function — you only place this element yourself if
you want to control placement or pre-render a region for SSR. Each region
is identified by its `placement`; calls to `toast()` are routed to the
region matching their requested placement, or a new region is created.
---


### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Toast elements. Normally populated by `toast()`; manual children are allowed and remain in DOM until dismissed.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Region anchor point. Default `block-end-inline-end` (bottom-right).

slots.description: Toast elements. Normally populated by `toast()`; manual children are allowed and remain in DOM until dismissed.

elements.name: cui-tree
elements.description:
Hierarchical tree with selection, expand/collapse, keyboard navigation,
and optional drag-and-drop reordering. Each item is a light-DOM
`.cui-tree-item` with a unique `data-id`; nested `<ul>`s become subtrees.
Consumers handle persistence — the element fires events but does not
mutate item structure on its own.
---


### **Events:**
 - **cui-tree-item-select** - Fires when an item is selected. `detail.id` is the selected `data-id`.
- **cui-tree-contextmenu** - Fires on right-click / context-menu key. Call `detail.preventDefault()` to suppress the native menu and render your own.
- **cui-tree-dragstart** - Fires when a drag begins.
- **cui-tree-drop** - Fires on a valid drop. `detail.position` is `before`, `after`, or `inside`. Consumers apply the structural change in their event handler.
- **cui-tree-expand** - Fires when a subtree expands.
- **cui-tree-collapse** - Fires when a subtree collapses.

### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Tree markup: nested `<ul>` / `<li class="cui-tree-item">` with `data-id` on each item and optional `.cui-tree-toggle` controls for expand/collapse.

attributes.name: selectable
attributes.description: Whether items can be selected. Default true.

attributes.name: draggable-items
attributes.description: Enables drag-and-drop reordering. Consumers must handle the resulting `cui-tree-drop` event to persist the new structure.

slots.description: Tree markup: nested `<ul>` / `<li class="cui-tree-item">` with `data-id` on each item and optional `.cui-tree-toggle` controls for expand/collapse.

events.name: cui-tree-item-select
events.description: Fires when an item is selected. `detail.id` is the selected `data-id`.

events.name: cui-tree-contextmenu
events.description: Fires on right-click / context-menu key. Call `detail.preventDefault()` to suppress the native menu and render your own.

events.name: cui-tree-dragstart
events.description: Fires when a drag begins.

events.name: cui-tree-drop
events.description: Fires on a valid drop. `detail.position` is `before`, `after`, or `inside`. Consumers apply the structural change in their event handler.

events.name: cui-tree-expand
events.description: Fires when a subtree expands.

events.name: cui-tree-collapse
events.description: Fires when a subtree collapses.

events.name: cui-tree-item-select
events.description: Fires when an item is selected. `detail.id` is the selected `data-id`.

events.description: Fires on right-click / context-menu key. Call `detail.preventDefault()` to suppress the native menu and render your own.

elements.name: cui-navbar
elements.description:
Responsive site navigation with collapsible mobile panel, nested dropdowns,
and optional sticky positioning. The element renders no styling of its own —
it enhances light-DOM markup built from the `.cui-navbar` class family,
injecting the burger toggle button and wiring up collapse, dropdown, sticky,
and ARIA behavior. All brand/nav/action content stays in the light DOM so it
remains semantic, searchable, and SEO-friendly.

The burger button is the one piece of chrome the component owns: it is
created and ARIA-wired by the element rather than authored, so the
`aria-expanded` / `aria-controls` contract can't be mis-authored per page.
Everything else is plain `.cui-navbar-*` markup.
---

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description: Mobile collapse panel state. Toggled by the injected burger button; can also be set programmatically.

attributes.name: sticky
attributes.description: Enables `position: sticky` on the navbar.

attributes.name: sticky-offset
attributes.description: CSS length used as `top` while sticky (e.g. `0`, `var(--cui-space-2)`).

attributes.name: sticky-z-index
attributes.description: CSS `z-index` while sticky.

elements.name: cui-scroll-stage
elements.description:
Scroll-driven "stage" element that publishes its focus/offset/active state
as CSS custom properties on itself (and optionally on a tone target like
`:root`), so child layouts and parallax effects can react via CSS alone.
Pair with `cui-reveal` for entry animations or with the exported
`attachCuiParallax()` helper for parallax bindings.
---


### **Slots:**
 - _default_ - Stage content. Typically a sticky inner region plus the layers that move with scroll progress.

attributes.name: focus-bias
attributes.description: Where in the viewport the stage is considered "in focus". CSS length or unit-less ratio.

attributes.name: hold
attributes.description: How long to keep the stage at full focus before easing out, as a CSS length or ratio.

attributes.name: threshold
attributes.description: Minimum viewport overlap before the stage starts publishing focus (0–1).

attributes.name: tone
attributes.description: Tone value to publish on the tone target while the stage is active (e.g. `dark`, `inverse`).

attributes.name: tone-target
attributes.description: CSS selector that should receive the tone attribute. Accepts `document` or `:root` to target the root element.

attributes.name: sticky-top
attributes.description: CSS `top` value applied to the inner sticky region.

attributes.name: min-block-size
attributes.description: Minimum block-size of the stage; controls how much scroll the stage consumes.

slots.description: Stage content. Typically a sticky inner region plus the layers that move with scroll progress.

pseudo-elements.name: part(viewport)
pseudo-elements.description: The clipping region around the slides.

pseudo-elements.name: part(track)
pseudo-elements.description: The slot wrapping the slides (the moving track).
